steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: In any triangle, all three angles sum to 180°. With ∠R = 63°: ∠S + ∠T = 180° − 63° = 117°.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: For a valid triangle, every angle must be greater than 0°. So ∠T = 117° − ∠S must be positive, which means ∠S < 117°.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Check each option: A) 116° $\Rightarrow$ ∠T = 1° > 0 (valid). B) 118° $\Rightarrow$ ∠T = −1° (impossible). C) 126° $\Rightarrow$ ∠T = −9° (impossible). D) 180° $\Rightarrow$ ∠T = −63° (impossible). Only A works. That is the correct answer.

choiceElimination:
• Choice A — $116$: ∠T = 117° − 116° = 1° > 0°. All three angles (63°, 116°, 1°) are positive and sum to 180°. Valid triangle. ✓
  • Choice B — $118$: ∠T = 117° − 118° = −1°. A negative angle is impossible in a triangle. ✗
  • Choice C — $126$: ∠T = 117° − 126° = −9°. A negative angle is impossible in a triangle. ✗
  • Choice D — $180$: ∠S alone equals 180°, which would account for the entire angle sum by itself, leaving no room for ∠R or ∠T. Impossible. ✗
That is the correct answer.
